verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase canonically localized to the cytoplasm and associated with the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. While baseline localization is cytosolic/inner-leaflet, LYN has documented surface display in specialized contexts: (1) B-cell and mast-cell activation-induced translocation to the outer leaflet during receptor signaling and degranulation; (2) cancer-cell ecto-kinase display — oncogenic transformation relocates LYN and other Src-family kinases to the outer face where they function as ecto-kinases; (3) immunogenic cell-death-related externalization. These non-baseline surface pools qualify as contextual surface accessibility via cell-state and activation-induced mechanisms.
